notes: Covered by tests/contracts/qc-workflow-boundaries.test.mjs for cap, voided status, UOM, receipt_allocations mutation, lifecycle transitions, reject->NCR, and replay.
Verified after the createQcEvent workflow split; receipt_allocations mutation and evaluateQcCommand behavior remain on the receipt-specific path.
Covered by tests/contracts/qc-workflow-boundaries.test.mjs for existence, UOM, capped quantity when quantity_completed is non-null, null bypass, and replay.
Verified after refactor that quantity_completed = null still bypasses the quantity cap.
Covered by tests/contracts/qc-workflow-boundaries.test.mjs for existence, UOM, quantity cap, and replay.
Verified after refactor that execution-line quantity cap enforcement still holds.

Covered by tests/contracts/qc-workflow-boundaries.test.mjs for warehouse existence, QA source, SELLABLE/HOLD destination, transfer execution, audit behavior, keyed replay, and current unkeyed behavior.
Verified that audit logging now runs inside the transfer transaction and keyed replay no longer duplicates qc_accept audit rows.
Verified for receipt, work order, execution line, and warehouse disposition via tests/contracts/qc-workflow-boundaries.test.mjs.
Workflow-specific replay handlers remain explicit even where they currently share the same replay helper.
Keyed replay confirmed for all QC workflow families covered by the QC workflow contract file; unkeyed warehouse disposition remains non-idempotent.
Warehouse disposition transactional audit callback only executes for newly created movements, preserving replay/idempotency semantics.
Guard failures in warehouse disposition remain zero-partial-write failures.
Warehouse disposition now uses withInventoryTransaction to own an explicit transaction boundary.
transferInventory + recordAuditLog are both within the same withInventoryTransaction call, ensuring atomicity.
Explicit transaction model replaces transactionalSideEffect callback; no behavioral drift from prior Sprint 2 Phase B verification.
New contract test: 'warehouse disposition transfer and audit log are co-committed within the same explicit transaction boundary' added to tests/contracts/qc-workflow-boundaries.test.mjs.
Standalone transferInventory and transferInventory with an external client both delegate to executeTransferWithClient.
executeTransferWithExternalClient was removed; the shared executor owns claim, replay, lock, execute, event append, projection ops, and finalize.
tests/contracts/transfer.test.mjs asserts persisted-state parity for success, replay, rollback, and ordering.
tests/contracts/manifest.json now includes tests/contracts/transfer.test.mjs so transfer parity runs in the contracts tier.
No receipt_allocations, Work Order NULL bypass, Execution Line QC cap, or QC workflow behavior changed.
runInventoryCommand no longer exposes executeWithClient and has one execution lifecycle model again.
Standalone transferInventory uses private runTransferCommandWithClient inside transfers.service.ts for its transfer-scoped SERIALIZABLE retry boundary.
runTransferCommandWithClient is not exported and the transfer contract includes a negative boundary test proving other modules cannot access it through service exports.
Transfer contract coverage now explicitly verifies idempotency lifecycle completion across standalone, external-client, and replay paths.
Architecture guards permit the private transfer runner only for transferInventory and keep other movement entrypoints on runInventoryCommand.
qc_event audit behavior remains covered by the QC workflow contract file.
Warehouse disposition keyed replay now leaves qc_accept audit row count at one for the authoritative movement.
